summary: Energetic second set with stellar Werewolves and Dancing In The Street
blurb: The show shines on the encore’s Werewolves of London and a tight Dancin’ In The Street, while the Scarlet > Fire transition glows in the matrix source. Jerry delivers soaring vocals on Peggy‑O and Stella Blue, and the Drums segment lifts the second set. Overall performance swings between inspired jams and occasional vocal slip‑ups.
review: On May 11, 1978 at the Springfield Civic Center, the Dead offered a night that oscillated between raw, chaotic energy and moments of pure brilliance. The first set was uneven – a flat opening that only sparked with highlights like a crisp Cold Rain And Snow and a spirited Peggy‑O – but the second set surged forward. A seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain transition captured in the matrix recording is a standout, and the band’s playful detour into a Fire/Dancin’ In The Street jam kept the vibe high. The encore delivered one of the night’s most memorable moments: an exuberant Werewolves Of London that had Garcia “letting loose” on guitar and vocals, while the Drums segment provided a loose‑but‑exciting percussive lift. Jerry’s vocal peaks on Peggy‑O and Stella Blue were praised across recordings, though his delivery faltered on a few verses of Stella in the soundboard source. Phil’s bass growls anchored the Werewolves groove, and Bob’s chemistry with Donna Jean added extra fire to Supplication and Dancing In The Street. Despite some sloppy moments, the setlist’s high‑energy peaks make this show worth revisiting.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, two are mixed; reviewers consistently praise Werewolves of London, Dancing In The Street, Scarlet>Fire transition and the Drums segment while noting occasional vocal slippage. This moderate‑positive consensus yields a +1.0 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard upgrade delivers crisp, balanced audio that captures the highlight moments (Werewolves, Peggy‑O, Drums) despite some performance unevenness.

key_highlights: Werewolves of London encore with fiery Garcia guitar and vocals
Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain transition shines in matrix source
High‑energy Dancin’ In The Street and Cold Rain And Snow set the tone
Loose but exciting Drums segment lifts the second set

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers soaring vocals on Peggy‑O and Stella Blue, and his guitar shines on Werewolves; occasional vocal slip‑ups noted in the soundboard source.
band_performance.Phil: Provides solid bass foundation with notable growls during Werewolves of London.
band_performance.Bob: Offers strong rhythm work, wild vocals on Dancing In The Street, and a fiery lead on Lazy Lightning/Supplication; chemistry with Donna Jean adds extra spark.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums segment is a highlight—loose yet exciting, driving the second set’s momentum.
band_performance.Billy: Together with Mickey, creates a fun, loose percussion backdrop that elevates the show’s energy.
